name: sparse-merkle-tree
title: Sparse Merkle Tree with Poseidon Hash for Enterprise ZK Validium State Management
hypothesis: A Sparse Merkle Tree of depth 32 with Poseidon hash can support 100,000+ entries with insertion latency < 10ms, Merkle proof generation < 5ms, and proof verification < 2ms in TypeScript, while maintaining full compatibility with the BN128 scalar field for Circom circuit integration.
null_hypothesis: A depth-32 Sparse Merkle Tree with Poseidon hash cannot meet all three latency targets simultaneously at 100,000+ entries, or introduces unacceptable memory overhead (>2GB) or incompatibility with BN128 field arithmetic.
predictions: Poseidon hash will be 5-10x faster than MiMC in native JavaScript execution
Insert latency will scale O(log n) with tree depth, not entry count
Memory usage will be sublinear due to sparse storage (only non-empty nodes stored)
Proof size will be exactly 32 * hash_output_size bytes for depth-32 tree
